scope.summary:
Goal: Implement a multi-section sidebar panel in the native egui frontend that ports the React WorkspaceSidebar pins/favorites/bookmarks sections plus adds backlinks and unlinked mentions panels for the currently-open block. The panel has five collapsible sections: Pins, Favorites, Backlinks, Unlinked Mentions, and a breadcrumb strip. All data comes from the real PostgreSQL backend via existing loom view APIs. Expected diff shape: one new file src/frontend/handshake_native/src/loom/sidebar_panel.rs containing LoomSidebarPanel, edits to src/frontend/handshake_native/src/loom/mod.rs.

Exact files to create or edit:
- CREATE src/frontend/handshake_native/src/loom/sidebar_panel.rs — LoomSidebarPanel widget
- EDIT src/frontend/handshake_native/src/loom/mod.rs — pub mod sidebar_panel; + re-export

Reuse existing WP-011 modules (do not reinvent): the loom graph surface lives in src/frontend/handshake_native/src/loom_graph.rs — reuse its LoomBlock/LoomEdge types and graph-view integration for backlinks rendering. Change-notification after pin/favorite mutation must be emitted through the existing shared event bus at src/frontend/handshake_native/src/event_bus.rs (not a bespoke channel). AccessKit node registration reuses the existing accessibility helpers under src/frontend/handshake_native/src/accessibility/ (mod.rs, live.rs, registry.rs, snapshot.rs). HTTP calls go through the shared backend client at src/frontend/handshake_native/src/backend_client.rs.

Exact APIs / React reference:
- PRIMARY REACT REFERENCE: app/src/components/WorkspaceSidebar.tsx — the Bookmarks section (lines 363-424) calls queryLoomView(workspaceId, 'pins', {limit:100}) and renders bookmark rows with open/remove actions. The setLoomBlockPinOrder + updateLoomBlock({pinned:false}) pair is the remove-bookmark flow (WorkspaceSidebar.tsx lines 293-312). The 'favorites' view type (LoomViewType, api.ts line 1058) is the favorites surface.
- SECONDARY REACT REFERENCE: app/src/harness/loom_bookmarks_harness.tsx — the integration harness wiring WorkspaceSidebar + LoomBlockPanel.
- Backend bindings:
  - GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/loom/views/pins?limit=100 — LoomViewResponse {view_type:'pins', blocks:LoomBlock[]} (queryLoomView 'pins')
  - GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/loom/views/favorites?limit=100 — LoomViewResponse {view_type:'favorites', blocks:LoomBlock[]}
  - GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/loom/views/unlinked?limit=50 — LoomViewResponse {view_type:'unlinked', blocks:LoomBlock[]}
  - PATCH /workspaces/{workspace_id}/loom/blocks/{block_id} — updateLoomBlock({pinned:false}) to unpin
  - PUT /workspaces/{workspace_id}/loom/blocks/{block_id}/pin-order — setLoomBlockPinOrder(null) to clear pin order
  - PATCH /workspaces/{workspace_id}/loom/blocks/{block_id} — updateLoomBlock({favorite:false}) to un-favorite
  - GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/loom/blocks/{block_id}/backlinks — load backlinks for currently-open block (rich doc backlinks, api.ts line 3326 for documents; for loom blocks use graph-search with mentionIds=[block_id])
  - GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/loom/graph-search?mention_ids={block_id}&limit=50 — load loom backlinks/mentions for active block

Step-by-step implementation notes:
1. Define LoomSidebarPanel { workspace_id: String, active_block_id: Option<String>, pins: Vec<LoomBlock>, favorites: Vec<LoomBlock>, backlinks: Vec<LoomBlock>, unlinked: Vec<LoomBlock>, breadcrumbs: Vec<BreadcrumbEntry>, on_open: Callback<String>, loading_section: HashSet<SectionKind>, error_section: HashMap<SectionKind, String> } where SectionKind = Pins | Favorites | Backlinks | Unlinked.
2. On workspace_id change, reload Pins and Favorites sections. On active_block_id change, reload Backlinks and Unlinked sections.
3. Breadcrumb strip: maintain a Vec<BreadcrumbEntry { block_id, title }> as navigation history. Each on_open callback push adds an entry. Render as a horizontal strip at top: 'Home > Folder > Block'. Each crumb is clickable (on_open fires for that block_id). Truncate to last 5 crumbs. AccessKit: each crumb 'sidebar.breadcrumb.{idx}' role=Link.
4. Pins section: render as a collapsible section header 'Pins (N)'. Each pin row: block title + kind chip + 'Remove' button. Remove = call PUT /pin-order (null) then PATCH {pinned:false}; remove from local list immediately (optimistic) and re-fetch. AccessKit: each row 'sidebar.pin.{block_id}', remove button 'sidebar.pin.{block_id}.remove'.
5. Favorites section: same pattern as Pins but calls PATCH {favorite:false} to remove. AccessKit: 'sidebar.favorite.{block_id}', 'sidebar.favorite.{block_id}.remove'.
6. Backlinks section: render only when active_block_id is set. Show blocks that mention the active block via GET /loom/graph-search?mention_ids={block_id}. Each row shows source block title + edge_type label. Click -> on_open(source_block_id). AccessKit: 'sidebar.backlink.{block_id}'.
7. Unlinked mentions section: render only when active_block_id is set. Show blocks from GET /loom/views/unlinked that textually mention the active block title but have no edge. Click -> on_open(block_id). AccessKit: 'sidebar.unlinked.{block_id}'.
8. Section headers are collapsible (egui::CollapsingHeader or manual toggle bool). Each section has a Retry button on error.
9. Emit a 'handshake:loom-bookmarks-changed' equivalent event through the existing event_bus.rs so other panels can react to pin/favorite changes.

Dependencies / unblock conditions: MT-021, MT-022, MT-023 must be complete so that src/frontend/handshake_native/src/loom/mod.rs is fully scaffolded and the shared backend_client.rs HTTP client is available. Cross-WP note: the existing loom graph surface (loom_graph.rs), event bus (event_bus.rs), accessibility helpers (accessibility/*), and backend client (backend_client.rs) are delivered by WP-KERNEL-011 and are reused here, not recreated.

Risk if missed: The sidebar navigation surface is absent. Users cannot find pinned blocks, favorites, backlinks, or unlinked mentions, which are core Obsidian-parity knowledge-navigation features.
acceptance_criteria: AC1: The Pins section loads all pinned LoomBlocks from a real PG workspace (>=2 pins seeded). Block titles and kind chips display correctly.
AC2: Clicking 'Remove' on a pin row calls PUT /pin-order(null) then PATCH {pinned:false}, the row disappears from the list, and a re-fetch confirms the block is no longer pinned.
AC3: The Favorites section loads all favorited blocks; removing a favorite calls PATCH {favorite:false} and the row disappears.
AC4: With an active_block_id set, the Backlinks section loads and displays blocks that mention that block via the graph-search API (>=1 backlink in test data).
AC5: With an active_block_id set, the Unlinked Mentions section loads and displays unlinked blocks.
AC6: The breadcrumb strip shows the navigation history (up to 5 entries). Clicking a breadcrumb fires on_open with the correct block_id.
AC7: AccessKit tree contains 'sidebar.pin.{block_id}', 'sidebar.favorite.{block_id}', 'sidebar.backlink.{block_id}', 'sidebar.breadcrumb.{idx}' nodes as appropriate.
AC8: Sections collapse and expand via their section headers. Collapsed section does not show its rows in the AccessKit tree.
AC9: Backend error on any section shows an error message with a Retry button in that section only; other sections remain functional.
proof_targets: PROOF1: cargo test -p handshake-native loom::sidebar_panel -- --nocapture passes unit tests for breadcrumb push/truncate logic (max 5 entries).
PROOF2: kittest test with 2 pinned blocks in real PG: asserts 2 'sidebar.pin.*' AccessKit nodes in Pins section.
PROOF3: kittest remove-pin test: click 'sidebar.pin.block-001.remove', assert PUT /pin-order and PATCH requests fired, assert 'sidebar.pin.block-001' no longer in AccessKit tree.
PROOF4: kittest backlinks test: set active_block_id='block-A' (which has 1 loom edge pointing to it), assert 1 'sidebar.backlink.*' node in AccessKit tree.
PROOF5: kittest breadcrumb test: open 3 blocks in sequence via on_open, assert 3 'sidebar.breadcrumb.*' nodes in order.
implementation_notes: Use separate loading booleans per section (HashMap<SectionKind, bool>) so sections load independently and a slow Backlinks fetch does not block Pins from rendering.
Optimistic remove: immediately remove the row from the local Vec on remove-click; if the backend PATCH fails, re-add the row and show an error inline.
Breadcrumb strip: render horizontally using egui::ui.horizontal(). Each crumb is an egui::Button with label truncated to 20 chars if long.

Backlinks vs unlinked: Backlinks = blocks with a real LoomEdge to active_block_id (query mention_ids). Unlinked = blocks returned by /views/unlinked — these are blocks the backend has identified as having textual mentions but no edge. The distinction is important; do not mix the two lists.
Pin removal: must call both PUT /pin-order(null) AND PATCH {pinned:false} in sequence (same as React WorkspaceSidebar.tsx lines 297-298). Do not skip the pin-order clear.
Collapsible sections: store expanded state per SectionKind in a HashMap<SectionKind, bool>; default all to true (expanded).

Emit change notification after pin/favorite mutation through the existing shared event bus (src/frontend/handshake_native/src/event_bus.rs) so other panels (e.g. the loom graph view in loom_graph.rs) can react.

Reuse the WP-KERNEL-011 loom graph surface (src/frontend/handshake_native/src/loom_graph.rs) for LoomBlock/LoomEdge types and graph integration; reuse accessibility helpers (src/frontend/handshake_native/src/accessibility/*) for AccessKit node registration; route all HTTP through src/frontend/handshake_native/src/backend_client.rs.
ports_from_react: app/src/components/WorkspaceSidebar.tsx — entire Bookmarks section (lines 363-424), handleRemoveBookmark (lines 291-312), loadBookmarks using queryLoomView 'pins' (lines 91-109)
binds_backend_api: GET /workspaces/{workspace_id}/loom/graph-search?mention_ids={block_id}&limit=50 — load backlinks for active block

risks: RISK-1: Removing a pin requires two sequential API calls (PUT pin-order + PATCH pinned=false); if the second fails the block is in a partially-unpinned state. Mitigation: always issue both; on second failure, re-fetch the block to determine true state and update UI accordingly.

RISK-2: Backlinks and Unlinked sections make additional API calls on every active_block_id change, potentially causing a surge of concurrent requests when the user navigates quickly. Mitigation: debounce active_block_id changes by 150ms before firing section reload.
RISK-3: Breadcrumb history grows unbounded if on_open is called many times in a session. Mitigation: cap at 5 entries, dropping oldest.

RISK-4: Unlinked mentions may include the active block itself or blocks already shown in Backlinks. Mitigation: filter unlinked list to exclude active_block_id and block_ids already in backlinks list.
RISK-5: Section collapse state is lost on panel rebuild (egui re-creates widgets). Mitigation: store collapse state in widget-level persistent state using egui::Memory::data keyed by a stable Id.
minimum_controls: MC-1: Two-call pin removal with error recovery and re-fetch on failure. Covered by AC2 and PROOF3.
MC-2: 150ms debounce on active_block_id changes before section reload. Covered by AC4.
MC-3: Breadcrumb capped at 5. Covered by PROOF5.
MC-4: Unlinked dedup vs backlinks. Covered by AC5 (separate lists).
MC-5: Collapse state in egui::Memory. Covered by AC8.
